prompt: I need to build a Python CLI tool that takes a folder of CSV files and converts them into a single, well-formatted Excel workbook with one sheet per CSV, a summary sheet with row counts and column names, and a bar chart on the summary sheet showing row counts per file. I'm using Python 3.12 and have pandas and openpyxl available. The CSVs can have different schemas. Output to a path the user specifies with --output. Make this production-ready.

assertions.text: Agent classifies this as Complex and explicitly runs full 5-phase RPIRT rather than doing it directly
A research artifact (rpirt/01-research.md or equivalent) is created with findings about relevant libraries and edge cases
A plan artifact (rpirt/02-plan.md or equivalent) is created with durable decisions and task breakdown
Step 4 review generates a domain-specific rubric with coded criteria (Correctness, Error Handling, UX, etc.) and scores them
The final Python CLI code is complete and not pseudocode (has actual import statements, argument parsing, and logic)
Edge cases are explicitly addressed: empty CSVs, missing --output flag, CSVs with different schemas
